findings.evidence: grep hits: `Weight.lean:62:@[expose] noncomputable def characteristicWeightNumerator` and `Weight.lean:94:@[expose] noncomputable def characteristicWeight`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions; add any missing characteristic lemmas instead of exposing bodies.
findings.issue: `characteristicWeightNumerator` and `characteristicWeight` are marked `@[expose]`, making their bodies part of the public interface even though the file provides `characteristicWeightNumerator_def`, `characteristicWeight_def`, and `two_mul_characteristicWeight`.

findings.evidence: Existing plumbing basis API uses `@[simp] theorem intersectionForm_single`; the new grep hits are unannotated at `Weight.lean:148:theorem characteristicWeightNumerator_single` and `Weight.lean:168:theorem characteristicWeight_single`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the single-vector normal-form lemmas that should be used as the public reduction rules.
findings.issue: The basis-vector normal forms `characteristicWeightNumerator_single` and `characteristicWeight_single` are untagged, so simp cannot reduce the new API on the plumbing basis without unfolding.

summary: The weight API exposes implementation bodies and leaves basis-vector normal forms out of simp. Tighten the public surface and annotations before merging.
